bullets.text: Combined ratio was 94.6 for Q1 2008, up from 89.5 in Q1 2007, ending 17 consecutive quarters below 90 as prior rate reductions flowed into earned premium.
Over 80 product/state rate revisions were implemented in Q1 as earned premium per vehicle fell 5-6% year-over-year; Progressive prefers early rate action to allow faster correction.
Personal Auto policies grew 1%, Special Lines 8%, and Commercial Auto 6% versus the prior year, with sequential monthly growth in all product lines during the quarter.
Agency personal auto declined 2% while Direct grew 7%; Agency accounts for ~62% of policies and the Internet was the fastest-growing source of new business.
Personal Lines written and earned premiums fell 4% and 3%, respectively, reflecting period-over-period average premium reductions; Agency written premium lagged prior year by 6%.
Investment total return was negative 2% for the quarter; unrealized gains declined over $500 million from year-end 2007 due to exposure in preferred stocks and mortgage-backed bonds.
Progressive recognized $50.2 million of other-than-temporarily impaired securities into income in March; management stated comfort with portfolio allocation but pledged continued diligence.
Customer retention focus was validated by the University of Michigan's American Customer Satisfaction Index naming Progressive most improved across all industries surveyed.
Progressive hired a new Chief Marketing Officer, launched a new TV campaign with thematic continuity, and announced a $10 million title sponsorship of the Progressive Automotive X PRIZE.
Progressive announced entry into Massachusetts on May 1st, completing availability in all 50 states, initially through the Direct channel with an Agency product to follow.
